touches.ai_summary: Cold call attempt to Noah Gupta at GoldenStack. No answer, left voicemail introducing AskElephant's pipeline visibility solutions for EdTech sales teams.
LinkedIn connection request sent to Noah Gupta with personalized note about helping EdTech sales teams gain pipeline visibility. No response received.
Cold email to Noah highlighting pipeline visibility challenges common in EdTech sales. Noah responded expressing interest in learning more about how we help directors track deal progression.

questions_asked.question: Do you integrate with HubSpot?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we have native HubSpot integration plus API connections to most major CRMs.

next_steps: Schedule intro call to discuss GoldenStack's pipeline visibility needs

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up call attempt to schedule discovery meeting. Noah did not pick up, left voicemail referencing his email response.
Email sent with calendar link and brief overview of pipeline visibility features. No response received within 48 hours.
Follow-up email with case study of similar EdTech company improving forecast accuracy. Noah replied confirming interest and booking discovery call for next Tuesday.
next_steps: Discovery call scheduled for Tuesday 2pm ET

touches.ai_summary: 33-minute discovery call with Noah Gupta. Discussed GoldenStack's current pipeline challenges: manual reporting, limited visibility into deal health, difficulty forecasting with 40-person team. Noah manages 6 AMs and needs better insights. He's interested in automations + consulting package. Currently using basic HubSpot reporting.

questions_asked.question: What's your current process for weekly pipeline reviews?
questions_asked.answer: Mostly manual - my AMs update a shared spreadsheet and we review on Mondays. Takes about 3 hours total each week.

questions_asked.question: Who else would need to be involved in evaluating a solution like this?
questions_asked.answer: Mainly me and my team. Our VP of Sales would want to see it eventually, but I can make this decision for my team.

questions_asked.question: How long does implementation typically take?
questions_asked.answer: For your team size, usually 2-3 weeks with our consulting package included.

objections: We've looked at tools before and they always require too much manual data entry from reps
next_steps: Include Yuki Torres (AM) in next meeting

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email with technical documentation and pricing overview. Noah confirmed receipt and scheduled technical eval for following week with Yuki Torres joining.

questions_asked.question: Can you send the case study you mentioned for the other EdTech company?
questions_asked.answer: Attached the case study showing 40% reduction in pipeline review time for similar-sized sales team.

next_steps: Technical eval scheduled with Noah and Yuki

touches.ai_summary: 32-minute technical evaluation with Noah and Yuki Torres (Account Manager). Walked through live demo of pipeline visibility dashboard, automation workflows, and HubSpot integration. Yuki asked detailed questions about daily workflow and data accuracy. Both were engaged and took notes throughout.

questions_asked.question: How does the AI determine which deals are at risk?
questions_asked.answer: We analyze activity patterns, stakeholder engagement, and deal velocity compared to your historical win/loss data.

questions_asked.question: Can individual AMs customize their own dashboard views?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, each user can create custom views while directors see aggregate team data.

questions_asked.question: What would success look like for you in the first 90 days?
questions_asked.answer: Cutting our Monday pipeline review time in half and having confidence in our monthly forecast numbers.

next_steps: Noah to discuss with his AM team
Schedule follow-up to review pricing and timeline

touches.ai_summary: 28-minute executive alignment meeting. Noah walked through business case internally but VP of Sales did not join this call. Discussed ROI calculation: 3 hours/week saved on reporting = ~$15K annually. Noah wants to move forward with 9 seats (him + 6 AMs + 2 growth slots). Reviewed implementation timeline and consulting support.

questions_asked.question: Has your VP seen the demo or materials? When can we get them involved?
questions_asked.answer: I've briefed her but she's swamped with our board meeting prep. I have budget authority for this level, so we can move forward.

questions_asked.question: What's included in the consulting package vs the base automations tier?
questions_asked.answer: Consulting adds dedicated implementation support, custom workflow building, and quarterly optimization reviews.

touches.ai_summary: Email with formal proposal for $8,300 ACV automations + consulting package, 9 seats. Noah acknowledged receipt and mentioned he needs to review the pricing with his team before final approval.

questions_asked.question: Is there flexibility on the per-seat pricing if we commit to annual?
questions_asked.answer: The proposal already reflects our annual pricing. We have some flexibility on professional services hours if that helps.

next_steps: Follow-up call scheduled to discuss terms

touches.ai_summary: 34-minute negotiation call with Noah. He's pushing for a discount to $7,500 ACV citing budget constraints this quarter. Discussed value of consulting package vs base tier. Noah mentioned his VP is asking questions about ROI timeline but still hasn't joined a call. He wants to close this month but needs to see movement on price.

questions_asked.question: Would it help to have a conversation with your VP directly to address her ROI questions?
questions_asked.answer: She's really busy - I think if we can get to the right number I can get her approval without another meeting.

questions_asked.question: What if we started with 7 seats instead of 9?
questions_asked.answer: We could do that but the per-seat rate would actually be slightly higher on smaller volume.

objections: Budget is tight this quarter - we've already committed to other tools
next_steps: Check with leadership on pricing flexibility

touches.ai_summary: 19-minute pricing call with Noah. Offered 10% discount to $7,470 ACV if he can close by month-end with annual payment upfront. Noah seemed hesitant, said he needs to confirm with his VP but timeline might slip to next quarter. Asked about month-to-month option instead of annual commit.

questions_asked.question: Can we do a quarterly commit instead of annual?
questions_asked.answer: Our pricing is structured for annual commitments. We could explore quarterly but it would be at a higher effective rate.

questions_asked.question: What's driving the timeline concern? You mentioned wanting to close this month earlier.
questions_asked.answer: Just want to make sure we're making the right call. My VP wants to see this before we sign anything.

next_steps: Noah to schedule time with VP
